evals.prompt: /agentmaster-plan add per-user rate limiting to the Express API in this repo
evals.expected_output: A plan whose Toolchain section records npm/Node commands with manifest evidence (no Python assumptions); evidence gathered only via scout/code-analyst dispatches with a ledger snapshot at .agentmaster/ledger.md; disjoint parallel groups; Shared resources section; execution contract as the first plan lines; open questions surfaced.
assertions: Plan contains a Toolchain section whose commands come from package.json or CI config, cited by file
Transcript shows zero direct Read/Grep/Glob/Bash calls by the orchestrator
Every parallel group's file set is disjoint from every other group's
The plan's first lines are the agentmaster-execute execution contract
A ledger file exists at .agentmaster/ledger.md and plan claims cite ledger entries
A Shared resources section names owners or SERIALIZE markers

evals.prompt: /agentmaster-plan --headless fix the flaky TestCheckoutRetry failure in this Go service
evals.expected_output: A headless run: no AskUserQuestion calls; Go toolchain commands recorded; ambiguous decisions recorded as ASSUMED with rationale in Open Questions, or a BLOCKED: report if no safe default exists.
assertions: No AskUserQuestion tool call appears in the transcript
Toolchain section records go test/vet or equivalent with evidence
Each unresolved decision is marked ASSUMED with a rationale, or the output is a BLOCKED: report naming the human decision needed

evals.expected_output: Both groups dispatched as implementers in one message; the serialized-tagged verification is run by a scout after group completion, not by an implementer; at least one verification per group independently re-run; the run chains into agentmaster-review and ends with a verdict and a cost appendix.
assertions: Implementer dispatches for independent groups occur in a single message
The task tagged verification: serialized is verified by a scout dispatch, not inside an implementer report
At least one per-group verification is re-run independently of implementer claims
agentmaster-review is invoked after gates pass
The output ends with a cost appendix and .agentmaster/telemetry.md is written

evals.expected_output: Against a branch seeded with a missing authorization check and a duplicated helper: the authz gap is an accepted security finding at blocker or major; the duplication is a structure finding capped at major; any speculative-generality suggestion is rejected under the YAGNI counter-rule; verdict FIX REQUIRED with fix tasks in plan-task format.
assertions: The missing authorization check is an accepted finding on the security axis
The duplicated helper is an accepted structure finding with severity no higher than major
At least one rejection cites the YAGNI counter-rule or evidence, and no finding is accepted without a ruling
Verdict is FIX REQUIRED and fix tasks include file ownership and verification commands

evals.prompt: can you plan this refactor out for me?
evals.expected_output: agentmaster-plan does NOT auto-invoke (disable-model-invocation). Claude either plans conversationally or suggests running /agentmaster-plan explicitly; the elevated pipeline never starts on its own.
assertions: The agentmaster-plan skill is not invoked in the transcript
The response either handles the request directly or recommends /agentmaster-plan as an explicit next step

evals.prompt: /agentmaster-plan fix the typo in the README heading
evals.expected_output: The proportionality gate fires: the orchestrator recommends skipping agentmaster (or takes the lite path) instead of running the full ceremony — no multi-batch evidence gathering, no multi-group plan.
assertions: The response recommends making the change directly or uses the lite path
No more than one evidence dispatch occurs
No multi-group parallel plan is produced
